messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.

Sort the attendance numbers for all games in the 1975, 2013, and 2018 championship series involving the Boston Red Sox from lowest to highest. What is the second highest attendance in this sorted list

content.message:
I need to find the attendance numbers for championship games involving the Boston Red Sox in 1975, 2013, and 2018, then sort them from lowest to highest and find the second highest.

First, I need to identify the Boston Red Sox entity in Wikidata. Then I need to find championship games involving them in those specific years, and retrieve the attendance data.

Let me start by searching for the Boston Red Sox entity in Wikidata.

tool_calls.name: search_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 entity alternatives:
1. Boston Red Sox (wd:Q213959), also known as "BoSox", "Boston Americans", "redsox", "Redsox", "Red Sox", etc.:
  - baseball team and Major League Baseball franchise in Boston, Massachusetts, United States
  - is instance of baseball team
2. Category:Boston Red Sox (wd:Q8308077):
  - is instance of Wikimedia category
  - Wikimedia category
3. Template:Boston Red Sox (wd:Q6723938):
  - is instance of Wikimedia template
  - Wikimedia template
4. history of the Boston Red Sox (wd:Q11144150, matched via "Boston Red Sox history"), also known as "Boston Red Sox history", "the Boston Red Sox history":
  - history of one of the original franchises of the American League
  - is instance of aspect of history
5. WikiProject Boston Red Sox (wd:Q21829642):
  - is instance of WikiProject
  - Wikimedia subject-area collaboration
6. Boston Red Sox coaches (wd:Q4948049):
  - is instance of Wikimedia list of persons
7. Category:Boston Red Sox players (wd:Q8308086):
  - is instance of Wikimedia category
  - Wikimedia category
8. Category:Boston Red Sox managers (wd:Q8308056):
  - is instance of Wikimedia category
  - Wikimedia category
9. Template:2018 Boston Red Sox (wd:Q57913374):
  - is instance of Wikimedia template
  - Wikimedia template
10. Category:Boston Red Sox seasons (wd:Q8308055):
  - is instance of Wikimedia category
  - Wikimedia category

content.message:
I've found the Boston Red Sox entity (wd:Q213959). Now I need to find championship games involving this team in 1975, 2013, and 2018.

First, I need to identify what properties relate to championship games or series. Let me search for properties related to "championship" or "series" in the context of sports.

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. winner (wd:P1346 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "champion"), also known as "1st place medalist", "awardee", "champ", "champion", "has winner", etc.:
  - winner of a competition or similar event, not to be used from ... qualified with "for work" (P1686)) nor for wars or battles
2. points for (wd:P1358 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "championship points"), also known as "championship points", "competition points", "league points", "pf", "points", etc.:
  - number of points in a league table or decathlon. (Use P1351 for goals/points in a match)
3. Championat ID (wd:P10800 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "championat.com/"), also known as "championat.com/", "Championat.com ID", "Championat.com identifier", "Championat identifier", "https://www.championat.com/", etc.:
  - identifier of a team, sports facility, or player on the Championat website
4. league or competition (wd:P118 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "tournament"), also known as "competition", "cup", "division", "sports league", "tournament":
  - league or competition in which team or player has played, or in which an event occurs
5. award received (wd:P166 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "winner of"), also known as "award", "awarded", "awards", "awards received", "award won", etc.:
  - award or recognition received by a person, organization or creative work
6. competition won (wd:P2522 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "winner of"), also known as "contest won", "event won", "winner of", "won", "won event":
  - competition or sports event won by the subject
7. competition class (wd:P2094 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "class for competition", "compclass", "disability sport classifications", "qualification class", "qualifies for", etc.:

8. sport (wd:P641 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "play", "plays", "sport played", "sports":
  - sport that the subject participates or participated in or is associated with
9. number of matches played/races/starts (wd:P1350 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "races"), also known as "appearances", "caps", "games pitched", "games played", "gp", etc.:

10. sports discipline competed in (wd:P2416 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "sport event"), also known as "discipline of sport", "event in sports", "sport discipline", "sport discipline competed in", "sport disciplines competed in", etc.:

content.message: I need to find properties related to games or attendance. Let me search for properties related to "attendance" or "game attendance".

tool_calls.result:

6. aircraft registration (wd:P426 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "registration"), also known as "reg", "registration", "tail number":
  - identifier assigned to an individual aircraft by civil aircraft registry
7. count of students (wd:P2196 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "enrollment"), also known as "count of pupils", "enrollment", "number of pupils", "number of pupils or students", "number of students", etc.:

8. commercialization date (wd:P5204 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "availability date"), also known as "availability date", "date of commercialization", "marketed", "product release date", "release date":

9. announcement date (wd:P6949 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "date announced"), also known as "announced date", "announced in", "announced on", "date", "date announced", etc.:

  - significant or notable events associated with the subject

content.message: Great! I've found that "attendance" (wd:P1110) is the property I need for game attendance. Now I need to find properties related to championship series or specific game events. Let me search for properties related to "series" or "championship series".

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. collection (wd:P195 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "series"), also known as "archival holdings", "archives", "art collection", "bibliographic collection", "book collection", etc.:
  - art, museum, archival, or bibliographic collection of which the subject is part (item is in the collection of X)
2. part of the series (wd:P179 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "series"), also known as "collection", "episode of", "in series", "issue of", "listed in", etc.:
  - series which contains the subject
3. number of seasons (wd:P2437 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "no. of series"), also known as "no. of seasons", "no. of series", "number of series", "seasons":
  - number of seasons a television or radio series has had
4. series ordinal (wd:P1545 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "series number"), also known as "index", "num.", "number", "ordinal", "ordinal number", etc.:
  - position of an item in its parent series (most frequently a ... "ranking" defined as a property for evaluating a quality)
5. volume (wd:P478 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "number of series"), also known as "numbering in series", "numbering of part", "numbering of volume", "number of part", "number of series", etc.:
  - volume of a book or music release in a collection/series or a published collection of journal issues in a serial publication
6. room number (wd:P7532 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "suite"), also known as "apartment", "room identifier", "suite", "unit":
  - number or identifier of a room, which can qualify the address (P6375) of a place to complete it
7. list of episodes (wd:P1811 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "episode list"), also known as "episode list", "episodes list":
  - link to the article with the list of episodes for this series
8. season (wd:P4908 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "television series"), also known as "series of television program", "television season", "television series", "TV season", "TV series":
  - season of a series (television, web, podcast)
9. original broadcaster (wd:P449 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "channel"), also known as "aired on", "airs on", "broadcast on", "channel", "first air channel", etc.:
  - network(s) or service(s) that originally broadcast a radio or television program
10. number of episodes (wd:P1113 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "episodes"), also known as "episode count", "episodes", "no. of episodes", "series length":
  - number of episodes in a film/TV/radio series

8. production date (wd:P2754 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "year of production"), also known as "date of production", "filming date", "filming time", "year of production":
